title: Sunbeam
content:
I pray to the sunbeam from the window -
It is pale, thin, straight.
Since morning I have been silent,
And my heart - is split.
The copper on my washstand
Has turned green,
But the sunbeam plays on it
So charmingly.
How innocent it is, and simple,
In the evening calm,
But to me in this deserted temple
It's like a golden celebration,
And a consolation.
meaning:
Anna Akhmatova's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Sunbeam"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
